title: Stepping into your investment property
subtitle: A visualisation for welcoming property and investment ownership with openness and ease.
excerpt: A short visualisation from Day 47 of the MONEY program. The scene is a property you own, or are about to own. The practice makes the feeling of ownership familiar before it is real.
sourceNotes: Inspired by Day 47 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Future-pacing visualisation is a widely-used technique in performance coaching and NLP.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 47 of the MONEY program. One of the quiet barriers to investment is that ownership doesn't feel real until it happens, and by then the fear has already made the decision harder than it needed to be. This practice lets you walk through the feeling of ownership before the paperwork arrives.
Sit or lie comfortably. Close your eyes. Take three slow breaths and let the to-do list go quiet. You are not making a decision right now. You are just visiting a place.
Picture yourself standing outside a property. It does not have to be precise, a building, a space, a door. What you know, in the scene, is that it is yours. You are holding the key.

Open the door and walk in. Notice what the space feels like. Is it quiet? Light? What does it smell like? You are not inspecting it. You are arriving in it. Let the feeling of being there settle into your body.

Stand in the centre of the space for a moment. This is a place that generates income. It works for you while you sleep. The money it makes is quiet and consistent. You did not have to earn it today. It arrived because you made a good decision.
Let yourself feel what it is like to be someone who has done this. Not someone who hopes to one day. Someone who already has.
Take one slow breath and let the image fade. Open your eyes. The feeling you just had is available to you, bring it into any property-related decision you make today.
Inspired by Day 47 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Future-pacing visualisation is a widely-used technique in performance coaching and NLP.
